126.title: incident_documentation
126.content: A sudden CPU surge in the notification service caused delayed notifications and inconsistent delivery behavior across the project. The response team reduced load on affected instances, adjusted scaling limits, and monitored queue health to prevent message loss. Service behavior improved after traffic balancing, and the team is continuing performance tuning to reduce recurrence risk.

127.title: incident_documentation
127.content: A compute spike in a core backend service briefly degraded throughput and increased request latency during active delivery work. Engineers stabilized capacity, throttled high-cost execution paths, and validated recovery through sustained health checks. The service is now stable, and follow-up actions focus on preventing similar load concentration during future releases.

128.title: incident_documentation
128.content: Intermittent timeout errors in a dependent service created short-lived failures in downstream platform flows. The team isolated unhealthy instances, tuned retry behavior, and tightened timeout handling to improve resilience under burst traffic. Platform operations returned to normal, with additional monitoring in place to catch early regressions.

129.title: incident_documentation
129.content: An ongoing CPU pressure event in a critical service is actively impacting delivery confidence for dependent work. Incident responders are isolating expensive runtime paths, applying targeted scaling changes, and validating system behavior after each mitigation step. Work remains focused on restoring stable performance and reducing risk to blocked project streams.

130.title: incident_documentation
130.content: Authentication instability in the checkout path introduced intermittent sign-in and session validation failures. The investigation traced error patterns to recent configuration changes, and the team prepared controlled rollback and guardrail options while tightening alert thresholds. Active mitigation continues with emphasis on restoring consistent authentication behavior before broader rollout activity resumes.
